prompt: Who has the authority to decide if observers will be used for a game?
choices: Only the captains of the competing teams.
The captains or the event organizer.
explanation: According to rule 22.A, observers may be used if desired by the captains or the event organizer.

prompt: During an observed game, two players are arguing over a foul call and cannot reach a resolution in a timely manner. Neither player has asked the observer for help. What may the observer do?
choices: The observer must wait until one of the involved players explicitly requests their input.
The observer may step in and resolve the dispute without a request from the players.
The observer must issue a Spirit of the Game infraction to both players for delaying the game.
The observer must force the disc to go back to the thrower without resolving the foul.
explanation: Under rule 22.B.2.b, an observer may resolve a dispute without a request from the players involved if they cannot resolve it in a timely manner.

prompt: What is the fundamental agreement players make when playing a game under observers?
choices: They agree to abide by the observers’ decisions.
They agree to forfeit their right to make any calls on the field.
They agree that observers will call all fouls and violations actively.
They agree to defer all line calls to the opposing team if the observer's view is blocked.
explanation: Rule 22.C states that by playing under observers, the players agree to abide by the observers’ decisions.

prompt: An observer assesses a yardage penalty against Team A through the misconduct system. Team B decides they want to decline the enforcement of this penalty. Under what condition might the observer deny Team B's declination?
choices: If the observer believes the teams are trying to circumvent mandatory tournament rules, guidelines, or player safety.
If the penalty was assessed during the last five minutes of a closely contested game.
If Team B has already declined a previous yardage penalty in the same half.
The observer cannot deny the declination; teams always have the final say on yardage penalties.
explanation: Rule 22.D allows players to decline enforcement of a yardage penalty, but observers may deny the declination if they feel the teams are trying to circumvent mandatory tournament rules, guidelines, or player safety.
